generated_exposure.exposure_text: I get why people worry about strategic autonomy, but let's be real - we're already seeing how over-reliance on domestic production for critical tech is backfiring. Look at the semiconductor shortages last year - if we'd had more diverse supply chains, we wouldn't have been in this mess. The EU's trying to force local production of everything, and it's just making their economy less flexible. We need smart autonomy, not this blind nationalism that's actually weakening our position. Remember when the Biden admin tried to force rare earth processing here? Total disaster. We should be focusing on maintaining our alliances and keeping our options open, not cutting ourselves off from global markets.
